item.title: 美国扩大对伊朗打击行动引发海湾地区更多报复
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United States bombardedIranagain on Monday after announcing the death of another American service member, and Iran retaliated by launching attacks on American allies Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain, home of the U.S. Navy’s 5th Fleet.
Even as the U.S. and Iraninch closer to all-out waragain, glimmers of hope appeared on the diplomatic front, as Iran’s interior minister traveled to Pakistan, a key mediator in the conflict, for talks.
Theinterim dealsigned last month that was meant to end the fighting has crumbled. Shipping through theStrait of Hormuz— a vital supply route for world energy supplies — has largely stalled. And as fighting intensifies, both sides have targeted civilian infrastructure relied on by millions of people.
A new potential threat to world energy markets emerged Monday afterIran-backed rebels in Yemensaid they planned to prevent Saudi Arabia from shipping in the Red Sea, an alternate route for oil exports during the war.
The escalation has pushed oil prices higher in recent weeks. Benchmark Brent crude traded Monday above $88 a barrel and regular gasoline in the U.S. climbed to an average of $4 a gallon, keeping pressure on Americans’ wallets ahead of midterm elections this fall.
The U.S. militaryidentified two soldiers who were killedin Jordan in attacks that left a third person missing. Separately, the military confirmed another death in Iraq on Saturday during the “controlled detonation” of a downed Iranian drone. President Donald Trump took to social media on Monday to warn that “Every time Iran kills an American Soldier they will pay for that killing many times over!”
Trump was planning to attend a ceremony on Tuesday evening at Dover Air Force Base, where at least one service member’s remains were due to arrive.
The U.S. military’s Central Command said its ninth straight night of airstrikes targeted “Iranian military command centers, air defense and coastal surveillance sites, maritime capabilities, missile and drone launch sites and communications networks.” Last week, the U.S. struck bridges and a tower at an Iranian port.
Iran’s state-run IRNA news agency reported that the American strikes killed at least one person around Tabriz, a northwestern city about 520 kilometers (325 miles) from the capital, Tehran. Tabriz is believed to host underground missile bases run by Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard.
U.S. strikes likely also hit Bandar Imam Khomeini in Khuzestan province, Sirik and Jask in Hormozgan province and Konarak and Chahbahar in Sistan and Baluchistan province, IRNA reported.
The British military’s United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center said Monday evening that a second ship had been attacked a day earlier in the Strait of Hormuz, this one off the coast of the United Arab Emirates.
The other vessel caught fire in the Strait of Hormuz after being hit by a projectile near the coastline of Oman. The crew abandoned the vessel, which was adrift and still ablaze hours later, the UKMTO said.
The route around Oman has been the one the U.S. military has encouraged ships to travel to avoid Iran’s control. The Guard later claimed it was targeting tankers in the strait.
Tehran also hit U.S.-allied countries throughout the Middle East.
Kuwait said its air defenses fired at an incoming barrage, and Jordan’s armed forces said Monday evening they had shot down three Iranian missiles, adding that there had been no material damage or casualties. There was no immediate word from Iran.
Bahrain’s Foreign Ministry condemned Iranian drone strikes targeting the country’s air traffic systems, saying they endanger travel for civilians.
A new pressure point could emerge for world energy markets as Yemen’s Houthi rebels announced on Monday a maritime embargo against Saudi Arabia. The Houthis, who are backed by Iran, said they would block shipping between the Red Sea and the Gulf of Aden in response to an attack on Sanaa International Airport last week that they blamed on Saudi Arabia.
With the Strait of Hormuz blocked up, Saudi Arabia has been relying on a pipeline to the Red Sea to get millions of barrels of oil out to market. The Houthis earlier demonstrated their ability to disrupt shipping there when they targeted ships for months over the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza, with over 100 vessels attacked.
There was no immediate response from Saudi Arabia.
Trump has threatened to target Iran’s power stations and bridges to try to compel Tehran to loosen its hold on the Strait of Hormuz, through which one-fifth of world oil supplies were shipped before the war. Recent attacks suggest the U.S. military is carrying out that plan.
The U.S. in the past week reimposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports to halt its shipments of crude oil. The military says it has redirected seven ships and disabled one since then.
Pakistan has intensified diplomatic efforts in recent days to resuscitatethe interim ceasefirereached last month that called for resolving remaining issues tied to the war within 60 days. The truce unraveled after both sides resumed attacks on each other’s military installations and other infrastructure.
Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni arrived in Islamabad on Monday for two days of talks with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and others. Momeni’s counterpart, Pakistani Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi, expressed optimism.
“God willing, We will have good news,” Naqvi said.
On Sunday, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters that the U.S. is still open to negotiating with Iran but that “it has to be real.”
“If the door opens to diplomacy — if the guys that want to do something productive for Iran win and take control of that system, or take control of the negotiations — that’ll be a very positive development,” Rubio said. “That’s not where we are tonight, unfortunately.”
Iranian authorities on Sunday said at least 50 people have been killed and 517 wounded in the latest rounds of U.S. strikes. Since the war began on Feb. 28, 17 U.S. service members have been killed.
Associated Press writers Nasser Karimi in Tehran, Iran, Munir Ahmed in Islamabad, and Michelle L. Price in Washington contributed to this report.

item.title: 泽连斯基应对抗议活动时乌克兰向莫斯科发射 400 架无人机
item.description:
Ukraine fired more than 400 drones toward Moscow in its latest major attack on the Russian capital, the city’s mayor said Monday.
The blitz came hours afterRussia bombarded Kyivand other Ukrainian cities with ballistic missiles in a relentless cycle of tit-for-tat strikes that make a settlement to end Moscow’s more than 4-year-oldall-out invasionappear a distant possibility.
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, meanwhile, said he would hold “key talks” Monday as he tries to defuse a domestic political crisis, after last week’sgovernment reshuffleexposed a deep split between the military’s old guard and young innovators over how to fight the war.
Zelenskyy needs to reassure Ukrainians that sacrificing 35-year-old Mykhailo Fedorovas defense ministerand keeping faith with 60-year-old Soviet-trained Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as armed forces chief won’t derail the fight against Russia.
Days ofstreet protestsin favor of Fedorov and against Syrskyi followed Zelenskyy’s move.
Moscow Mayor Sergei Sobyanin said over 400 Ukrainian drones were sent toward the Russian capital from late Sunday until early Monday, with most downed far from the city and 85 intercepted near it.
Moscow regional Gov. Andrei Vorobyov said 10 people were wounded by Ukrainian drones, while several residential buildings and civilian infrastructure facilities were damaged.
The attack started a fire at the Yuzhnye Vrata industrial park, about 30 kilometers (18 miles) south of Moscow, according to Yevgeniya Khrustaleva, the head of the Domodedovo area of the Moscow region.
The Astra news outlet reported the assault also caused a fire at an oil depot in Podolsk, about 20 kilometers (12 miles) south of Moscow, but there was no official confirmation of the strike.
Ukrainian forces hit logistics facilities and an oil depot in the Moscow region, Zelenskyy said, adding that Kyiv’s forces hit two vessels belonging to Russia’s so-called shadow fleet transporting sanctioned oil and four dry cargo ships in the Black Sea.
A drone struck a passenger bus in Shebekino in Russia’s Belgorod region bordering Ukraine, killing five people — four women and a boy — and injuring 23 others, according to acting Gov. Alexander Shuvayev.
It was not possible to independently verify either side’s claims.
Ukraine’slong-range drone technologyhas evolved during the war, posing a problem for Russia, whose huge land mass is hard to protect fully. Kyiv’s forces have aimed especially at Russian oil facilities, causingfuel shortagesand embarrassing Russian President Vladimir Putin.
In May, the Russian Defense Ministry said air defenses downed1,054 Ukrainian dronesin a 24-hour period.
As Kyiv pleads with foreign partners to supply it withsophisticated interceptorsthat can counter Russian ballistic missiles, which are much harder to stop than drones or cruise missiles, Moscow’s forces have sought to take advantage of the weaknesses in Ukraine’s air defenses.
Ballistic missiles fly faster than the speed of sound and can reach Kyiv in minutes, giving air defenses little time to react and civilians to reach shelter.
Russia has stepped up itsuse of ballistic missilesrecently. Ukrainian officials said Sunday that Moscow’s bombardment killed at least six people and wounded dozens.
“Russian forces have launched more ballistic missiles against Ukraine thus far in July than Russia reportedly produces each month,” the Institute for the Study of War, a Washington-based think tank, said late Sunday.
“Russia appears to be dipping into its stockpiles to continue increasing the number of ballistic missiles it launches against Ukraine, as these missiles have a higher success rate than drones and cruise missiles,” it noted.
Ukraine’s air force said Russia fired two missiles and 94 long-range drones at Ukraine overnight. It said air defenses jammed or intercepted 81 drones, while nine drones and a missile caused damage at nine locations.
Russia’s Defense Ministry said Monday its forces struck fuel tanks in the southern Ukrainian port of Odesa overnight.
A Russian strike on Pavlohrad in Ukraine’s central Dnipropetrovsk region injured 13 people, according to military administration head Oleksandr Hanzha. Eleven people were hospitalized, including a 13-year-old girl, he added.
Ukraine’s Sea Ports Authority said a cargo vessel under the flag of the West African country of Guinea Bissau was hit by Russian forces on Sunday shortly after it left the port of Odesa, killing 10 of its crew, including five sailors from Syria. Eight people were rescued from the ship, which was carrying Ukrainian corn, it added.
Syria’s General Authority for Borders and Customs said it “strongly condemns the repeated attacks targeting commercial vessels with Syrian sailors and crews aboard in Ukrainian ports and nearby maritime areas in the Black Sea, and the resulting deaths and injuries among Syrian sailors.”
The Syrian statement did not mention Russia. Moscow has built relations with the new Syrian government since former President Bashar Assad was ousted in December 2024 in a rebel offensive led by now-interim President Ahmad al-Sharaa.
AP reporter Abby Sewell in Beirut contributed.
Follow the AP’s coverage of the war in Ukraine athttps://apnews.com/hub/russia-ukraine

item.title: 印度警方使用催泪瓦斯阻止蟑螂运动支持者在议会游行
item.description:
新德里（美联社）——警方使用催泪瓦斯和警棍阻止印度青年领导的蟑螂运动的支持者周一向议会游行，要求教育部长辞职，而政府似乎首次与该运动的领导人进行接触。
警方采取行动之前，数千名示威者试图冲破新德里市中心抗议地点周围的安全路障，这是该运动针对总理纳伦德拉·莫迪政府的运动中最严重的对抗之一。尽管遭到镇压，许多抗议者仍继续小群地向议会游行。
蟑螂简塔党发言人索拉夫·达斯指责当局“残酷镇压”“和平抗议者”。德里警方表示，这次游行并未获得授权，在周一开始的季风会议期间，需要采取限制措施来维持议会周围的法律和秩序。
距离议会仅几公里（英里）的简塔·曼塔天文台（Jantar Mantar）是指定的抗议地点，人群包括学生、专业人士和有小孩的家庭。随着时间的推移，集会蔓延到抗议地点之外，示威者挤满了周围的街道和道路。
许多人背着背包，里面装着水和食物，期待着漫长的一天。其他人挥舞着印度国旗，反对教育部长和莫迪的口号在越来越多的人群中回响。
最高法院首席法官在一次无关的听证会上将一些失业年轻人比作“蟑螂”后，于五月成立了“蟑螂人民党”。支持者们对这句话表示欢迎，将其变成了一场讽刺运动，在社交媒体上吸引了数百万粉丝。
在医学院和政府工作的高风险入学考试一再被泄露引发公众愤怒后，这一运动势头强劲。周六警方将绝食活动人士索南·旺楚克强行送往医院后，此事引起了更广泛的关注。
过去一个月，自称“蟑螂”的抗议者在简塔·曼塔天文台露营，几个学生团体的成员也加入了旺楚克的绝食抗议。数千名支持者也出现在其他城市的大学和集会上。他们要求教育部长达门德拉·普拉丹(Dharmendra Pradhan)辞职，改革考试制度，并对试卷泄露后自杀的学生家属进行赔偿。
莫迪政府的高级领导人对这场运动采取了强硬立场。普拉丹指责抗议者的行为是反国家的，而其他部长也承认学生的担忧，但坚称没有必要与该运动进行对话或谈判。
不过，周一该运动的代表与卫生部长 J.P. Nadda 举行会谈后，这种情况似乎发生了变化。这是政府向抗议领导人伸出援手的第一个明显迹象，抗议领导人试图直接向立法者提出他们的要求，促使当局加强了建筑群周围的安全。
会见纳达的 CJP 发言人达斯表示，他提出了抗议者的要求，包括立即辞职或罢免教育部长。
另外，活动人士旺楚克呼吁允许暂时离开医院并参加游行。
他还在一份手写的便条中表示，只有政府对教育系统的失败和试卷泄露承担责任，或者允许抗议者进入议会并且议员承诺在参众两院提出这些问题，他才会结束近三周的绝食。
这次抗议活动成为莫迪第三任期印度教民族主义政府罕见的公开挑战，得到了反对党、维权人士和一些宝莱坞名人的支持。抗议者表示，莫迪领导下的异议空间已经缩小，莫迪政府因监禁活动人士以及在某些情况下使用警察强力镇压来镇压公众抗议而受到批评。
瑜伽老师乔蒂·拉杰普特 (Jyoti Rajput) 表示，她参加抗议活动是因为“这个腐烂的制度拒绝采取行动。”
“它看不到学生的痛苦，这就是为什么这么多人加入这场运动，”她说。
对于其他许多人来说，这场运动已经扩大为对政府问责制的更广泛的抗议。一些人表示，他们对民选领导人缺乏透明度和反应能力感到沮丧。
26 岁的抗议者斯尼哈·埃皮利 (Sneha Eppili) 表示，她参加示威是因为她认为政府有责任回答他们的问题。
“我投票支持这个政府，所以我认为它有责任回答我们的问题。这是我应得的基本人权，”她说。

item.title: 当局称，在风景秀丽的犹他州峡谷发生山洪后，5名家庭成员被发现死亡
item.description:
犹他州比克内尔（美联社）——执法官员称，周五一场山洪席卷犹他州南部的峡谷，导致五名准备去徒步旅行的家庭成员丧生。
据消防官员称，遇难者是犹他州普罗沃消防局的一名队长、他的妻子和儿子。没有随行的女儿幸存下来。
韦恩县治安官办公室称，这家人于周五抵达犹他州比克内尔附近风景秀丽的红岩峡谷中的 Sunglow 营地，开始一日游。
治安官办公室在一份新闻稿中表示：“据信这家人是因大雨引发的洪水而罹难的。”
尽管治安官办公室没有透露遇难者的身份，但普罗沃消防局在社交媒体帖子中证实，消防队长斯宾塞·朗及其家人已遇难。
消防部门官员写道：“我们对斯宾塞·朗上尉及其家人的去世感到非常伤心。在这个困难的时刻，我们与他的家人、朋友和同事同在。”
周五晚上晚些时候，在洪水中发现一名年轻男性的尸体后，代表们被召集到露营地。其他家庭成员的尸体于周五晚和周六被发现。
与此同时，悼念之情涌入这个家庭。
据一位家庭朋友设立的 GoFundMe 网站称，朗、他的妻子卡特里娜以及他们的儿子里德、塞恩和盖奇在毁灭性的洪水中丧生，该网站是朗一家的朋友为支持朗一家的女儿，帮助她度过失去家人的痛苦。
“斯宾塞和卡特里娜是慈爱、忠诚的父母，他们最大的快乐就是家庭。他们的善良、慷慨和坚定不移的爱感动了许多人的生活，”布丽·尼尔森在页面上写道。
卡特里娜·朗 (Katrina Long) 是一家医疗水疗中心的联合创始人。峰会医疗水疗中心在 Facebook 上发帖称：“我们的心与他们所有的亲人同在，我们对卡特里娜、她的丈夫和三个漂亮男孩的失去感到悲痛。”

item.title: 职业拳击手在得克萨斯州乡村骑自行车时被​​车撞死
item.description:
德克萨斯州布赖恩（美联社）——一名正在崛起的职业拳击手在德克萨斯州的一条乡村道路上骑自行车，官员称一名驾车者从她身边驶过，停下来，然后倒车撞到了她，导致她死亡。
布拉索斯县治安官办公室在一份新闻稿中称，26 岁的汉娜·拉普 (Hannah Rapp) 周六早上与另一个人一起骑行时被撞。官员称，救援人员做出反应，将她送往附近的一家医院，但她在医院死亡。该地区位于休斯顿西北约 80 英里（130 公里）处。
世界拳击理事会主席毛里西奥·苏莱曼·萨尔迪瓦尔在一份声明中表示，整个国际拳击界都在哀悼拉普的去世。
“她是一位杰出的拳击手，但最重要的是，她是我们拳击大家庭的宝贵成员，”苏莱曼说。“我们与她的亲人、她的团队以及所有有幸在她辉煌的职业生涯中认识和支持她的人一起承受悲痛。”
拉普在印第安纳州约克镇长大，于 2022 年从普渡大学毕业。她于 2024 年搬到德克萨斯州，在德克萨斯农工大学担任消防和生命安全检查员。与此同时，拉普正在接受职业拳击手的训练，取得了8-0-1的战绩。拉普上个月在佛罗里达州奥兰多向蒂亚拉·布朗挑战 WBC 女子羽量级冠军，但以一致判定落败。
据治安官办公室称，31 岁的查尔斯·梅迪纳 (Charles Medina) 超越了拉普和车内的另一名骑车人，然后停车、倒车并撞到了拉普。调查人员没有立即提供动机。
梅迪纳被捕并被指控过失杀人罪。周日，他以 25 万美元的保释金被拘留。监狱和法庭记录没有列出他的律师。

item.title: 诺兰·威尔斯在艾尔·夏普顿牧师要求了解他的死亡真相时表现出的温柔精神被人们铭记
item.description:
密西西比州海泉市（美联社）——周一，诺兰·泽维尔·韦尔斯 (Nolan Xavier Wells) 的家人和支持者聚集在密西西比州的一座教堂，哀悼并庆祝这位 18 岁的孩子，阿尔·夏普顿牧师 (Rev. Al Sharpton) 恳求提供新证据，解决有关这名青少年 7 月 4 日失踪的“许多悬而未决的问题”。
葬礼在威尔斯父母泪流满面的讲话中开始，他们形容儿子是一个温暖而温柔的人，是弟弟妹妹的榜样，也是一位胸怀 NFL 雄心的天才运动员。
“他有办法让人们感到被关注、受到欢迎和受到重视，”他的母亲克里斯汀·旺斯利 (Christine Wonsley) 说。“他只需几句话就能提升你的信心，让你有归属感。”
在热烈的悼词中，夏普顿随后承诺悬赏 10 万美元，奖励任何有关威尔斯死亡情况的新信息。7 月 4 日，他与朋友乘船前往墨西哥湾沿岸的霍恩岛。
夏普顿指出，其中一半费用将由演员兼制片人泰勒·佩里承担，他还支付了葬礼费用。
“有人知道一些事情，”夏普顿继续说道，赢得了包括导演斯派克·李、前美国国家橄榄球联盟球员特雷尔·欧文斯和许多著名黑人神职人员在内的人群的掌声。“让每一块石头都被滚开，每一块面纱都被撕开，让真理像花朵一样从地里绽放出来。”
威尔斯是黑人，他和一群高中朋友一起去了霍恩岛——七月四日的一个受欢迎的目的地，他们都是白人。官员称，当晚他没有与朋友一起返回大陆。两天后，他的尸体在该岛海岸被发现。
杰克逊县警长约翰·莱德贝特表示，威尔斯的朋友正在配合调查，调查仍在进行中。治安官表示，初步采访显示威尔斯选择留在岛上，他的朋友相信他会与其他人搭车。
但导致威尔斯失踪的仍然未知的情况在网上引发了广泛的猜测，刺激黑人社区的领导人呼吁对他的死亡进行彻底和独立的调查。
夏普顿在悼词中强调，在获得更多信息之前，关注此案的人应暂缓做出判断。但他也质疑为什么威尔斯的手机和钥匙在他的朋友身上被发现，为什么他们在意识到他失踪后没有立即报警，以及为什么当地官员如此迅速地否认了谋杀的可能性。
夏普顿援引了密西西比州种族主义暴力的历史，包括对埃米特·蒂尔的私刑和对梅德加·埃弗斯的暗杀，他继续说道：“我们中的一些人还记得密西西比州发生的事情。在我们今天进行药物治疗和治愈之前，我们必须看看你们的历史。”
随后，韦尔斯家族的律师本·克伦普 (Ben Crump) 带领人群高呼“诺兰的生命很重要”，威尔斯的母亲伏在丈夫埃尔莫尔·旺斯利 (Elmore Wonsley) 的肩上抽泣。
“他们乘船出去，船回来了，两天后诺兰被冲到岸上死了，”克伦普说。“你不想得到答案吗？”
在葬礼后的新闻发布会上，克伦普表示，他们仍在等待独立尸检的结果。官方尸检结果也尚未公布。
克伦普此前表示，他正在与当地检察官一起检查威尔斯的手机。克伦普表示，检察官已同意在调查完成后向大陪审团提交调查结果。
威尔斯下个月就满 19 岁了，他在位于密西西比州萨米特的西南密西西比社区学院橄榄球队担任外接手，并渴望参加高水平的一级联赛。
在上周的一份联合声明中，国会黑人核心小组成员呼吁对他的死亡进行独立和透明的调查。
他们说：“我们希望最近几天全国人民倾注的关心和支持能给他们在未来的日子里带来一些安慰。”
美联社密西西比州杰克逊市的视频记者索菲·贝茨对此报道做出了贡献。

item.title: JD 和 Usha Vance 迎来了一个男婴，这是 150 多年来现任副总统所生的第一个孩子
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——副总统 JD 万斯和他的妻子、第二夫人乌莎·万斯周日宣布他们的第四个孩子出生，这是 150 多年来现任副总统的第一个孩子。
亚历克·尼尔·万斯 (Alec Neel Vance) 于周日出生，与 9 岁的哥哥伊万 (Ewan) 一起出生。维维克，6；和米拉贝尔，4。
万斯在社交媒体上宣布了这一消息，并发表了由他和妻子签署的声明。
声明称：“我们很高兴地宣布，我们的男婴亚历克·尼尔·万斯今天早上出生了。乌莎和孩子都很快乐、健康，我们的孩子很高兴见到他们的弟弟。”
他们感谢沃尔特·里德国家军事医疗中心以及白宫医疗团队的医生和工作人员。
这位共和党副总统不断壮大的家庭符合他对美国人生育更多孩子的热情倡导。他还表示，2025 年他的朋友、保守派活动家查理·柯克 (Charlie Kirk) 被杀是他决定再要一个孩子的因素之一。
前美国海军陆战队队员万斯在 2021 年开始竞选俄亥俄州参议员席位时多次对美国出生率下降表示担忧。作为副总统，他在华盛顿举行的 2025 年生命游行反堕胎集会上表示，“我希望美利坚合众国有更多婴儿。”
41 岁的副总统在 40 岁的乌莎·万斯 (Usha Vance) 和他们的孩子的陪同下出访海外，孩子们经常穿着睡衣登上空军二号进行夜间飞行。
印度移民的女儿乌莎·万斯 (Usha Vance) 已经因为她的丈夫而成为公众关注的对象。一月份宣布的她怀孕的消息进一步放大了人们的关注度，因为美国最高公职人员在任职期间很少有家庭成员加入。
上一次现任副总统成为新父亲是在 1800 年代。
据白宫历史协会称，1870 年，当科尔法克斯担任副总统时，斯凯勒·科尔法克斯和他的第二任妻子艾伦·韦德生了一个儿子，名叫斯凯勒·科尔法克斯三世。该协会称，几十年前，约翰·C·卡尔霍恩 (John C. Calhoun) 和他的妻子弗洛里德·博诺 (Floride Bonneau) 于 1829 年生下儿子威廉，当时卡尔霍恩担任副总统。
万斯家族的最新成员是唐纳德·特朗普总统政府高层最近出现的小型婴儿潮的一部分。白宫副办公厅主任斯蒂芬·米勒的妻子凯蒂·米勒最近生下了这对夫妇的第四个孩子，白宫新闻秘书卡罗琳·莱维特于五月与丈夫尼古拉斯·里奇奥生下了第二个孩子。
乌莎·万斯 (Usha Vance) 最近受到了密切关注，因为《纽约时报》的时尚评论家呼吁人们关注她在父亲节播出的“与第二夫人的故事时间”（她在怀孕期间推出的视频播客）节目中穿的紧身珊瑚色孕妇装。
《泰晤士报》写道，万斯、米勒和莱维特对怀孕的关注提供了“理想化女性形象，为生育运动提供了字面意义”，鼓励夫妇生育尽可能多的孩子，以应对出生率下降的趋势。
第二夫人在社交媒体上回应道，“现在我们知道了我价值 8.75 美元的 Old Navy 珊瑚色孕妇装的政治意义，迫不及待地想听听《纽约时报》对我的松紧腰带裤和压力袜的评价！同时，享受我的怀孕时尚（或缺乏这些时尚），并在 Storytime with the Second Lady 中与您的孩子们讲一个好故事。”后来她分享了这件衣服的收据，她的丈夫对她的节俭表示赞赏。
“她花了 8.75 美元买了一件 50 美元的裙子。美国：见见你的下一任联邦预算主任！”副总统在社交媒体上写道。
JD Vance 表示，是柯克的遗孀埃里卡·柯克 (Erika Kirk) 告诉他们，去年 9 月柯克在犹他州被暗杀之前，她希望自己能和丈夫有两个以上的孩子，这促使他和第二夫人决定扩大家庭规模。曾担任最高法院首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨 (John Roberts) 书记员的前律师乌莎·万斯 (Usha Vance) 部分同意这一观点。
“埃里卡（Erika）谈到她自己的家庭时所说的话非常有力，当然对我们俩来说都非常感动。我想我已经开始对这种可能性持开放态度，”乌莎·万斯在六月播出的哥伦比亚广播公司周日早间节目中与丈夫联合采访时说道。“无论如何，我不会说这对我来说是决定性因素。但它是在我们已经进行的对话中出现的。”
请关注美联社对副总裁 JD Vance 的报道：https://apnews.com/hub/jd-vance。

item.title: 特朗普 3.47 美元汽油背后的人：一名 NFL 教练、一名共和党筹款人和两名新泽西兄弟
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump and his White House have enthusiastically promoted the Freedom Fuel Network, a chain of star-spangled convenience stores selling gas at $3.47 per gallon in honor of the 47th president.
Untangling exactly who is behind the Philadelphia-area venture has proven difficult. Records indicate the chain, which was launched last month, is run by a disparate collection of businessmen that includes an NFL kicking coach, a GOP fundraiser and a New Jersey entrepreneur who this year was ordered, along with his brother, to pay civil damages for unlawfully taking more than 200,000 gallons of fuel.
How the stations got Trump’s attention remains a mystery, and the four businessmen declined to — or could not be reached for — comment.
What is clear: Trump loved the idea ofcheaper gas.The president celebrated the network’s gas prices just before the busy July 4th travel period as consumers were grappling with higher oil prices sparked bythe war with Iran.
“I am pleased to announce that a VERY smart Retailer, located throughout the Northeast, is stepping up,” Trump wrote about the company on his Truth Social platform on July 1. “America has never been stronger than it is now, and Gas Prices will soon be back to the Record Low Prices Americans enjoyed at the pump before our very successful ‘excursion’ in Iran.”
The White House followed up with a post on X a few days later that heralded the opening of the first Freedom Fuel station and produced a video showing patrons waving wads of cash and thanking Trump for reducing the prices outside a store festooned with American flags and a golden eagle logo.
That station,located in Dresher, Pennsylvania,is owned by a subsidiary of Blue Owl Capital, an investment firm, records show. Trump has owned up to $25 million worth of Blue Owl stock, though his most recent financial disclosure says he has sold almost all of that stake.
The White House denied Trump had any personal connection to the venture, but declined to say how the project was developed. It acknowledged having discussions with individuals who set up the network of gas stations.
“The Administration is not involved in the company, nor has the Administration given the company any funding. There is no other entity or person subsidizing the lower gasoline costs,” the White House wrote in a statement.
Blue Owl owns about a third of the Freedom Fuel properties, though the company said it leases the stores to independent contractors and “is not involved in the tenant’s operations or business decisions.”
Fourteen stations in the 25-location network are controlled by companies linked to Shamikh and Syed Kazmi, two brothers who have been dogged by a string of civil misconduct accusations, including fraud, records show.
Shamikh Kazmi is leasing eight of those stations from Blue Owl, according to state records and people familiar with the businesses who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss the matter. The Associated Press was able to link the brothers to six other Freedom Network locations through records that show they listed those stations’ addresses as headquarters for other ventures or supplied those locations with fuel.
A White House official, who insisted on anonymity to discuss the project, said that no one at the White House who was in conversations with Freedom Fuel Network had specifically spoken or worked with Syed Kazmi, a claim that indicates discussions occurred with the other brother, Shamikh.
The Kazmis have marketed themselves as “top tier” petroleum distributors and gas station operators, with over 75 years of experience and a deep well of corporate affiliations, according to an archived version of the website for one of their companies.
Public records offer a more nuanced portrait, showing the Kazmis have been repeatedly sued by companies they had dealings with.
AP’s global investigative team accepts tips securely and confidentially. Choose the method that works best for you.
Legal filings detail a series of judgments against the Kazmis, who failed to comply with a court order and have been accused of obscuring their finances and dodging service processors as a growing list of former suppliers and franchisors sought payment.
In February, a federal judge in New Jersey ordered the Kazmis to pay over $600,000 to a fuel supplier that accused the brothers of stealing gas. The supplier alleged in court filings that it cut the brothers off after they refused to sign a new contract. But the Kazmis exploited a security lapse and gained access to the supplier’s fuel depot. Over a ten-day period in August 2021, tanker trucks absconded with more than 230,000 gallons of fuel, according to the supplier’s court filings. A judge ruled in the supplier’s favor, finding the brothers unlawfully took the gas.
The fuel supplier says it has yet to receive payment.
Syed Kazmi was hit with a $380,000 judgment two years ago in a suit brought by 7-Eleven, the convenience chain, which accused him of “dishonest, unethical, immoral” conduct while operating a franchise in Lawrenceville, New Jersey, that was flagged for unsanitary conditions that included trash issues and a rodent infestation. The company also said “tens of thousands of dollars” of cigarettes ordered from 7-Eleven on credit had gone missing.
A federal judge held a company operated by Shamikh Kazmi in contempt in 2022 in a trademark case brought by BP America.
Though BP had severed ties with Kazmi’s Diwan Petrol two years prior to the legal action, the corporation’s signage had not been removed from the gas station despite a court order to do so. The judge authorized U.S. Marshals to accompany BP workers to remove it.
A man who answered a call to a number listed for the Kazmi brothers said he was not the right person to talk to and instead directed inquiries to the Freedom Fuel Network’s website. But that website has no contact information, phone number or mailing address. A request for comment submitted through an online contact form was not answered.
Records from Delaware, a state known for offering incorporators a large degree of opacity, show the Freedom Fuel Network was registered on June 23. The document forming the company was signed by Randy Brown and Yoni Gontownik.
Politicoand the websiteThe Newsgroundreported that Brown is asenior special teams coachwith the Baltimore Ravens. He has also served as the elected mayor of Evesham Township, a New Jersey suburb of Philadelphia, where a Freedom Fuel Network station is located.
Brown, a Republican, considered a run for Congress in 2021, telling a local newspaper he was a conservative and a “proud Trump supporter.”
Gontownik is a former investment director at Mercuria, a Swiss-owned commodities trading firm. He and his wife live in northern New Jersey and have been active with the pro-Israel political action committee NORPAC, including hosting fundraisers for Republican members of Congress.
Gontownik and Brown did not respond to requests for comment.
Jeff Lenard, a spokesman for the National Association of Convenience Stores, said Freedom Fuel’s rock bottom promotional price meant the chain was likely selling the gas at a loss.
“It’s not unusual for retailers to have prices that are different than a market when they’re looking to make a splash,” said Lenard, whose association’s members account for most U.S. retail fuel sales, adding that such a splash typically lasts “a matter of hours or a matter of days.”
Social media posts and gas-price checking websites show that the per-gallon rate advertised at Freedom Fuel locations began creeping up this week.
A Freedom Fuel station in Bensalem, Pennsylvania, for example, on Thursday was selling regular gasoline for $3.82 a gallon. That was 27 cents cheaper than at a Sunoco station across the street.
The Freedom Fuel Network posted anote of thanks to its websitethis week, crediting Trump’s “strong endorsement” for the “explosive growth” of their business.
“Despite the misinformation and baseless speculation circulating,” the statement reads, “let us set the record straight: Freedom Fuel Network is proudly lowering its prices to benefit our community.”

item.title: 夏奇拉、麦当娜和许多布偶登上了世界杯舞台，还有防弹少年团和比伯
item.description:
周日，麦当娜、贾斯汀·比伯、防弹少年团和夏奇拉在世界杯首场半场表演中各自带来了他们的热门歌曲，在旋风表演中，伯纳男孩、特德·拉索和许多布偶也纷纷亮相。
这场演出在纽约郊外的大都会人寿体育场举行，试图将超级碗的氛围带入世界杯，但这场 11 分钟的表演比通常的 13 分钟美式橄榄球表演要拥挤得多。这里的每位主角都花了大约两分钟的时间进行表演。
麦当娜以 2000 年流行舞曲《Music》拉开了这场演出的序幕，昔日的巴西足球明星罗纳尔多和罗纳尔迪尼奥也加入其中。比赛以在溜冰场上录制的一首明显的曲子开始，然后她骑着沙丘车进入体育场，唱着：“音乐，让人们聚集在一起。”
委内瑞拉指挥家古斯塔沃·杜达梅勒 (Gustavo Dudamell) 指挥管弦乐队演奏白色条纹乐队 (White Stripes) 的体育歌曲《七国军》(Seven Nation Army)，而木偶乐队 (Muppets house band)——包括鼓手 Animal (Animal) 代替梅格·怀特 (Meg White)——在背景音乐中提供了毛茸茸的震动。
韩国流行音乐界的轰动人物防弹少年团 (BTS) 在向挪威维京人划船致敬后，以红色和黑色的变奏形式登上了舞台，随后演唱了《Dynamite》。
贾斯汀·比伯 (Jason Sudeikis) 和布伦丹·亨特 (Brendan Hunt) 为即将到来的《泰德·拉索》(Ted Lasso) 季做宣传，为贾斯汀·比伯 (Justin Bieber) 打气，后者用原声吉他和轻松的感觉演唱了《Everything Hallelujah》，改变了节奏。
“好了小伙子，走吧！是你的大佬了！”苏戴奇斯对比伯说道。“出去吧，别紧张，没有人在看。”
夏奇拉 (Shakira) 和伯纳男孩 (Burna Boy) 随后演唱了他们的世界杯官方歌曲“Dai Dai”，这是本届世界杯所有 104 场开球前都会听到的歌曲。这位哥伦比亚超级巨星赤着脚，穿着黄色和粉色羽毛的连衣裙，周围是穿着类似服装的舞者——其中包括一群孩子——然后这位非洲节奏偶像加入了她的歌曲，歌曲的副歌是意大利版的“Let’s go！”
夏奇拉实际上已经将世界杯歌曲打造成了一种流派，其中包括 2010 年的“Waka Waka（This Time for Africa）”。
演出由史坦顿岛小学生组成的 PS22 合唱团拉开帷幕，杜达梅尔作为伴奏，策划演出的酷玩乐队歌手克里斯·马丁 (Chris Martin) 也加入了演出，还有夏奇拉 (Shakira) 以及更多布偶和《芝麻街》角色，包括克米特 (Kermit)、猪小姐 (Miss Piggy)、冯伯爵 (Count von Count) 和饼干怪物 (Cookie Monster)。
杜达梅尔和大约 40 名音乐家——大约一半来自纽约爱乐乐团，一半来自委内瑞拉西蒙·玻利瓦尔交响乐团——都在现场，尽管他们的音乐周三在曼哈顿中心预先录制。
随后，哈维尔·巴登和西班牙球迷一起在体育场庆祝他们的祖国第二次赢得世界杯。巴登一边跳舞一边挥舞着拳头庆祝胜利。
参加比赛的其他明星包括碧昂斯和 Jay-Z、蒂莫西·柴勒梅德和凯莉·詹纳，以及马特·达蒙，后者正在观看《奥德赛》史诗般的开场，度过了一个愉快的周末。他和妻子卢西安娜·巴罗索·达蒙 (Luciana Barroso Damon) 一起，她穿着祖国阿根廷的颜色衣服。
詹妮弗·哈德森、罗比·威廉姆斯等人表演了一系列的歌曲，波斯特·马龙带来了一首老歌和一首新曲，汤姆·克鲁斯在赛前闭幕式上发表了热情洋溢的炒作演讲。
英国歌手兼前 Take That 成员威廉姆斯穿着一身令人眼花缭乱的蓝色运动服，演唱了他的国际足联歌曲《欲望》。小野猫成员 Nicole Scherzinger 和身着蓝色亮片礼服的意大利歌手 Laura Pausini 也加入了他的行列。威廉姆斯一年前发行了这首歌，但本周早些时候与两位女士一起发布了新版本。
主播 IShowSpeed 首先上场，在帝国大厦的图像上表演了他的世界杯主题歌曲“Champions”。接下来是波斯特·马龙，他走上纽约/新泽西体育场的球场，球场上覆盖着防水布，可以看到纽约的天际线。他演唱了一首全新歌曲“Chrome Heartbreaker”，并与 Swae Lee 一起在场地中央的六边形舞台上表演了他们的 2018 年二重唱“Sunflower”。
克鲁斯也参加了巴黎奥运会闭幕式，随后穿着马球衫和牛仔裤发表了演讲。
“今天只剩下两支球队：西班牙队！”克鲁斯停下来欢呼，然后补充道：“阿根廷！”
“让我们庆祝一场将世界团结在一起的锦标赛，”他说。
大多数国歌都是在比赛期间播放的，但周日是现场演奏的。EGOT 获胜者、脱口秀主持人詹妮弗·哈德森演唱了《星条旗永不落》。她身穿红、白、蓝镶边的白色套装，站在一排世界国旗前，在铜管乐队的伴奏下清唱。
西班牙铜管乐队演奏了西班牙器乐曲《La Marcha Real》，随后是阿根廷的《Himno Nacional Argentino》，由流行歌星玛丽亚·贝塞拉演唱，她身着飘逸的连衣裙，让人想起阿根廷国旗。

item.title: 费兰·托雷斯如何打进世界杯制胜球帮助西班牙击败阿根廷
item.description:
新泽西州东卢瑟福（美联社）——费兰·托雷斯在世界杯上为西班牙队攻入制胜一球，这粒进球由队内两名最好的球员和两名在周日对阵阿根廷的决赛中替补上场的球员完成。
在那之前，西班牙队共射门 11 次，但未能攻破对方门将埃米利亚诺·“迪布”·马丁内斯。
“我们有很多机会，”西班牙教练路易斯·德拉富恩特说。“但迪布绝对是非凡的。”
两队比赛已经进行了 105 分钟，当恩佐·费尔南德斯在常规补时阶段吃到第二张黄牌被罚下时，西班牙队看起来占据了很大的优势。
尽管如此，在11人对10人的比赛中，西班牙队在加时赛上半场无法利用任何机会，包括在尼科·威廉姆斯将球送入球网之前，裁判裁定米克尔·梅里诺踩到了阿根廷后卫尼古拉斯·奥塔门迪的一只脚而导致的进球无效。
加时赛过半，西班牙队在中场得球，比分是0-0。阿根廷队由于人手不足，几乎没有做出什么抵抗。
保罗·库巴西 (Pau Cubarsí) 将球传给马克·库库雷拉 (Marc Cucurella)，然后是埃里克·加西亚 (Eric García)，最后传给马丁·祖比门迪 (Martín Zubimendi)，后者在右侧找到了亚马尔 (Yamal)。亚马尔在比赛一开始就射门入网，并在更早的时候获得了另一个进球机会，赢得了队友和教练的称赞，称他是19岁的场上最好的球员之一。
德拉富恩特说：“他踢出了伟大的足球，这种足球将帮助他成熟，甚至成为一名比现在更伟大的足球运动员。”“他确实为了集体利益而做出了牺牲，这对我们球队来说非常重要。”
亚马尔将球传给了佩德罗·波罗，后者在决赛中触球的次数几乎和其他人一样多。
这是波罗 130 次触球中最重要的一次，他做到了这一点。他的右脚传中到禁区，差一点就超出了远门柱。
第75分钟上场的威廉姆斯并没有越过球门线出局，而是将球头球顶向托雷斯。
正是在这个时刻，德拉富恩特回顾了安德烈斯·伊涅斯塔在加时赛中的进球，帮助西班牙赢得了2010年世界杯。
“那张照片、那个形象在我脑海中非常生动，”德拉富恩特说。
托雷斯在比赛刚过一小时时就上场，他的左脚完美配合。这是西班牙本场比赛的第 20 次射门，当时阿根廷队没有进球，第 106 分钟，西班牙队越过几名后卫和马丁内斯，帮助西班牙队以 1-0 领先。
“我认为归根结底，这是 4700 万人打进的进球，”托雷斯说道。“我感到很高兴。我在世界杯期间受到了严厉的批评，但我认为这是命运和事情写成的。但我总是有这样的机会，有继续下去的选择，上帝把它给了那些相信它的人，那些最值得的人。”
这位巴塞罗那球星在被周围庆祝的队友和球迷包围时踢倒了角旗。
“我很高兴他能打进这个进球，”德拉富恩特说道。“他将创造历史。他已经创造了历史，这是他应得的。”
德拉富恩特表示，西班牙球员和教练知道比赛还没有结束，试图保持冷静。
托雷斯险些再次破门，但越位，西班牙队抓住阿根廷队的角球机会，击败卫冕冠军，第二次夺得世界杯冠军。

item.title: 西班牙世界杯冠军回国受到皇家欢迎并在马德里举行游行
item.description:
马德里（美联社）——西班牙世界杯冠军足球队周一回国，这支由 26 人组成的球队受到了该国皇室成员的欢迎，然后会见了首相佩德罗·桑切斯。
随后，球队将登上一辆敞篷巴士，预计将在马德里市中心进行喧闹的游行。
当他们沿着马德里历史悠久的大道从总理官邸蒙克洛亚宫附近前往西贝莱斯广场时，数十万球迷将迎接他们。
随后，球员们将在广场上举行仪式，此前的西班牙球队及其支持者传统上都会聚集在这里庆祝。
周日在新泽西州东卢瑟福举行的决赛中，西班牙队以 1-0 击败了已被淘汰的冠军阿根廷队，捧起了奖杯。这是继 2010 年在南非获胜之后，该队第二次夺得冠军。
该队受到了共同主办本届赛事的三个国家领导人的祝贺——美国总统唐纳德·特朗普、墨西哥总统克劳迪娅·辛鲍姆和加拿大总理马克·卡尼。
终场哨响后，马德里爆发了庆祝活动，数千名支持者从酒吧和餐馆涌上街头，许多人披着西班牙国旗或穿着球队的颜色。
首都的太阳门广场和其他主要广场上挤满了人群，人们载歌载舞，在汽车喇叭鸣响的同时点燃照明弹。
许多人庆祝周日的胜利直到周一凌晨。
“我很高兴。我睡了三四个小时，但为了球队，我会不惜一切代价，”19岁的维克多·阿尔瓦雷斯说道。
男子队的胜利，再加上西班牙队在 2023 年夺得女子世界杯冠军，使这个欧洲国家成为第一个同时获得这两个冠军头衔的国家。
“说实话，这非常令人兴奋。我们宁愿不去任何酒吧或任何地方，而是在家里体验它，专注，非常高兴，非常快乐，”59岁的公务员杰马·罗德罗说。
然而，当地政府报告称，马德里西北部萨拉曼卡的一名 13 岁男孩与朋友爬上庆祝胜利的喷泉倒塌后死亡，庆祝活动受到抑制。
对于许多年轻的支持者来说，这场胜利是他们第一次见证男队举起奖杯，以及随之而来的全国狂欢。
现在的西班牙与2010年有明显不同，当时这个伊比利亚国家饱受债务危机、20%失业率和民族主义地区争取更多自治权的困扰。
经济正在扩张，失业率减半，预计该国将再次成为欧元区增长最快的主要经济体之一。民族主义和分离主义的紧张局势已大大降温。
周一上午晚些时候，在球队抵达之前，成群结队的球迷开始在西贝莱斯广场附近占据座位，他们携带雨伞来抵御阳光和高达 35 摄氏度（95 华氏度）的气温。

item.title: 对他们来说不是希腊语。学者质疑克里斯托弗·诺兰的《奥德赛》
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——对于希腊语翻译艾米丽·威尔逊来说，导演克里斯托弗·诺兰的《奥德赛》的上映在各个方面都令人欣慰。她广受好评的 2017 年版荷马史诗已跻身亚马逊网站前 5 名，引领了由该电影推动的类似书籍热潮。诺兰甚至引用了她的翻译，尤其是开场白“告诉我一个复杂的男人”作为灵感。
但这部电影本身并没有令她满意，该片在首映周末在全球获得了超过 2.6 亿美元的票房，并受到了许多影评人的好评。
她告诉美联社：“我想庆祝这样一个事实，即越来越多的人了解了《奥德赛》并重新认识了这首诗。”“但与此同时，我有一种感觉，克里斯托弗·诺兰试图将所有内容都包含在内，但却遗漏了这首诗所必需的主题。”
《奥德赛》是今年最受期待的影片之一，由马特·达蒙纳斯饰演归来的战士奥德修斯，安妮·海瑟薇饰演他的妻子佩内洛普，汤姆·霍兰德饰演他们的儿子特勒马科斯。评论家们对表演和叙事动力、完全用 IMAX 胶片拍摄的电影的宏大规模以及它如何将古希腊和现代世界的情感结合起来表示赞赏，至少诺兰是这么认为的。
学者和评论家之间的分歧似乎部分取决于天平应该向诺兰倾斜还是荷马倾斜。威尔逊和翻译同事丹尼尔·门德尔松都对诺兰的电影天赋和雄心表示钦佩，门德尔松称之为“伟大的视觉华丽”。两人都猜测他想要拍一部“克里斯托弗·诺兰”电影的愿望导致了不幸的决定。
门德尔松是一位作家、评论家和翻译家，他的评论发表在周五的《纽约书评》上，他是该书的特约编辑。
门德尔松此前曾为诺兰选择黑人演员露皮塔·尼永奥饰演特洛伊海伦这一颇具争议的决定进行辩护，称这是“激发、挑战和扰乱我们对美和身份的看法”的一种有价值的方式。但他指责导演给我们塑造的奥德修斯“没有聪明，没有幽默，没有狡猾的魅力”。他认为，西方经典中的一位塑造性骗子已经变成了诺兰之前的电影《奥本海默》和《记忆碎片》中那种闹鬼的主角。
门德尔松的《奥德赛》译本于 2025 年出版，他写道：“这个备受折磨、充满负罪感的奥德修斯，被剥夺了幽默和机智、诱惑和聪明，是《记忆碎片》中痛苦的失忆症患者、蝙蝠侠、奥本海默的兄弟，这些人都被过去的折磨所折磨，并以不同的方式与之抗争。诺兰只是按照自己的形象重塑了荷马的英雄。”
作家加雷斯·海因兹 (Gareth Hinds) 的图画小说改编版《奥德赛》也因这部电影而获得了销量增长，他对诺兰对奥德修斯的诠释也有类似门德尔松的反应。这个“狡猾的骗子和能说会道的人”现在是一个“看起来很正直的人，除了在某些特定的情况下，这些情况会继续困扰他。”对于海因兹来说，诺兰的电影提出了一个问题：在故事本身完全改变之前，你可以在多大程度上改变故事的主角。
“这是诺兰留给我们思考的许多有趣的事情之一，”海因兹说，他称赞导演创造了一部“强大的电影”。
宾夕法尼亚大学古典研究教授威尔逊表示，她不想给人留下“势利”、吹毛求疵的专家的印象，她不同意任何与原文的偏差。但她对一系列遗漏和其他选择提出了质疑，从宙斯和波塞冬诸神（雅典娜由赞达亚饰演）的缺席，到谴责战争和身体怯懦的“语无伦次”信息。她发现佩内洛普的角色如此淡薄，以至于危及“任何相信这段婚姻的可能性”。威尔逊还认为电影的某些部分，尤其是奥德修斯的狗阿尔戈斯的场景，“迎合了现代感伤”。
“每个人都是狗的傻瓜，”她说。
诺兰的发言人没有立即回应置评请求。威尔逊拒绝提供这部电影的评分或排名，但表示她会向其他人推荐这部电影，只要他们在剧院看到它。
“和我的孩子们在一起很有趣。我不觉得与它相处需要拔牙，”她说。“如果你能在大屏幕上看到它，你绝对应该去。”

item.title: 诺兰执导的《奥德赛》全球首映票房突破 2.641 亿美元
item.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Christopher Nolan’s“The Odyssey”launched with an estimated $124.5 million in domestic ticket sales and another $139.6 million from overseas, notching an even better debut than“Oppenheimer”and marking the filmmaker’s best opening since 2012’s “The Dark Knight Rises.”
Nolan flexed his singular box-office might with a global debut unlike any other. Few filmmakers alive could pull off astarry, big-budget adaptation of Homer’s epic poem. But in a Hollywood where intellectual property rights rule most hits, Nolan turnedone of the world’s oldest works of literatureinto an unlikely summer blockbuster.
The Universal production was no small gamble on Nolan, coming off the 2023best picture-winning “Oppenheimer.”With a production budget of $250 million, it’s among the most expensive R-rated movies ever made. Universal is spending some $125 million to market it.
But no behind-the-camera name turns out audiences more than Nolan’s. So great was the hype on “The Odyssey” that IMAX put tickets on sale for some 70 mm showtimes a full year in advance. To satisfy the extraordinary demand for Nolan’s preferred format, IMAX 70 mm, some theaters added midnight and 3 a.m. screenings — and sold them out.
“It’s incredibly rewarding to see the palpable excitement in theaters this weekend,” said Jim Orr, head of domestic distribution for Universal. “Nolan has crafted an epic adventure. It’s on a scale that audiences have responded to extraordinarily well.”
Billed as Nolan’s first feature shot entirely with IMAX cameras, the format drove a huge slice of ticket sales. That included $29.6 million domestically on IMAX and $51.8 million globally, leading to the company’s best weekend ever. IMAX is dedicating its screens entirely to “The Odyssey” for three weeks. Though only 41 IMAX screens can screen “The Odyssey” in 70 mm, they accounted for $6.3 million in ticket sales.
“The IMAX of it all helped turn it from not only a blockbuster, but a global cultural event,” said Rich Gelfond, chief executive of IMAX. “We have tickets on sale for week five in some theaters and some of them have already sold out.”
Since the pandemic, Nolan has been at the forefront of reviving cinemas. His“Tenet”was one of the first big releases to wade back into theaters in 2020. Three years later, “Oppenheimer” and Greta Gerwig’s “Barbie” combined to create arguablythe movies’ signature moment of the decade. “Oppenheimer” ultimately grossed $975 million worldwide.
“The Odyssey” arrived in theaters during Hollywood’s best summer since 2019. Ticket sales are running 10.4% ahead of last year, according to Rentrak. The industry is expecting the first $10 billion year at the domestic box office since the pandemic.
The only question for “The Odyssey” will be how front-loaded it is, given that many moviegoers have had this weekend circled for months. The film faced no new-release competition over the weekend, and it won’t next weekend, either.
Gelfond said that presales for the movie’s second weekend would rank among their 10 best presales, proof that many moviegoers are waiting to see the film in their preferred format. IMAX 70mm screenings, he said, are largely sold out for the next month, except for some front row seats.
“Christopher Nolan and Tom Cruise may be the two most high-profile ambassadors for the big-screen experience,” said Paul Dergarabedian, head of marketplace trends for Rentrak. “Nolan is a director who’s a star as much as any movie star in front of the camera.”
The audience for “The Odyssey” was notably male, accounting for 59% of tickets sold. But Orr said moviegoers were otherwise “ridiculously broad,” playing across demographics and geography.
“It’s delivering for a 17-year-old dude and it’s delivering for a female that’s 55 and up,” said Orr. “It points to what I’m convinced will be a very long, very successful run throughout not only the rest of the summer but into the fall, too.”
The next movie to pose any competition, ironically, also stars Tom Holland and Zendaya: Sony’s “Spider-Man: Brand New Day” on July 31.
“The Odyssey” stars Matt Damon as Odysseus and features Holland as his son, Telemachus; Anne Hathaway as Penelope; Zendaya as Athena; Robert Pattinson as the suitor Antinous and Charlize Theron as the sea nymph Calypso.
Nolan’s casting of “The Odyssey,” including Lupita Nyong’o as Helen and Elliot Page as a soldier, was controversial to some conservative commentators. Elon Musk called Nolan a “coward” over Nyong’o’s casting.
But that criticism had little to no effect on “The Odyssey” becoming one of thebig-screen cultural eventsof the year. Reviews (95% fresh on Rotten Tomatoes) are among the best of Nolan’s career. Audiences gave it an “A” CinemaScore.
PG-rated films followed in the wake of “The Odyssey.” The Walt Disney Co.’s“Moana”landed in a distant second place with $19 million in its third weekend. Following its disappointing $43 million launch, the live-action remake slid 56% on its second weekend. In two weeks it collected $178 million worldwide, a poor result for a film that cost $250 million to make.
“Moana” was trailed by Universal’s“Minions & Monsters”and Disney’s“Toy Story 5,”both of which grossed about $15 million.
With final domestic figures being released Monday, this list factors in the estimated ticket sales for Friday through Sunday at U.S. and Canadian theaters, according to Rentrak:

item.title: 随着美国和伊朗发动袭击，美国天然气价格再次回升至平均每加仑 4 美元
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——随着美国和伊朗发动更多袭击，美国天然气价格周一再次跃升至每加仑 4 美元的平均水平。
根据 AAA 汽车俱乐部联合会的数据，全国每加仑普通汽油的平均价格现已回到 4 美元。一年前的平均价格为每加仑 3.14 美元。
该价格为全国平均价格，这意味着一些州的司机一段时间以来支付的价格远高于每加仑 4 美元，而其他州的支付费用则较低。由于邻近供应和不同税率等因素，各州之间的价格有所不同。
世界各地的人们也在应对战争造成的高油价问题。
3 月底，平均汽油价格首次突破每加仑 4 美元。6 月中旬，油价跌破该水平，并随着美国和伊朗达成临时协议而导致原油价格下跌，油价继续下跌。即便如此，唐纳德·特朗普总统仍对天然气价格没有像油价下跌一样快表示失望。
负担能力可能是美国中期选举选民的一个关键问题，而天然气和石油价格上涨可能有助于推高食品杂货和其他商品的价格。
随着美国和伊朗接近恢复全面战争，油价近几天再次攀升。
周一美国早盘交易中，国际标准布伦特原油小幅下跌，下跌 1.64 美元，至每桶 86.46 美元。但这仍然远高于战前水平，当时原油价格接近每桶 70 美元。与此同时，美国基准原油价格下跌 1.87 美元至每桶 79.91 美元。

item.title: 法官表示派拉蒙和华纳必须停止合并至少两周，批准各州的请求
item.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Monday ordered Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery to halttheir $81 billion mergerfor at least two weeks, allowingstates that are challenging the dealmore time to see their case through in court.
Twelve states, led by California, sued to block Paramount’s pending buyout of Warner last week — alleging that such a combination would “extinguish competition” in Hollywood and lead to fewer choices for consumers, particularly moviegoers and cable customers across the U.S.
The states’ top prosecutors called on Warner and Paramount to not close the transaction until after a court had time to “fully evaluate” their claims. And when the companies refused, they filed for a temporary restraining order — which is what District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín granted on Monday. That opens the door to a potential preliminary injunction that the states are also seeking to effectively block the deal.
“This is a critical first win in our case to ensure this megamerger never sees the light of day,” California Attorney General Rob Bonta said in a statement following Monday’s order. “History tells the tale of what happens when a few people have great power over markets that are central to Americans’ lives: fewer opportunities for more people, worse products and services for all people.”
A Warner-Paramount tie-up would bring togethertwo of the five last legacy studiosin Hollywood — as well as host of TV networks, titles filling streaming libraries and news operations. Warner’s HBO Max, fan favorites like “Harry Potter” andeven CNNwould come under the same roof of Paramount-owned CBS, movies like “Top Gun” and the Paramount+ streaming service.
Paramount, which was bought outby Skydancejust last year, has vowed to “vigorously defend” its Warner acquisition — and touts regulatory greenlights the deal has received elsewhere, including fromthe administration of President Donald Trumplast month.
On Monday, Paramount said that the states’ antitrust arguments “are without merit” or “any basis in modern market realities” — and maintained that its merger with Warner was pro-competitive and with benefits to consumers and workers alike. Timing for next steps also falls in line with the company’s previous requests.
The temporary restraining order granted Monday halts the deal from progressing for at least 14 days, although the pause could be extended for up to 28 days. The court has set Aug. 3 as a date for a hearing on the states’ preliminary injunction motion, although that schedule could also be pushed back.
The clock is ticking. Ahead of Monday’s decision, the companies proposed wrapping up a preliminary injunction hearing by the end of August, leaving time to make a possible appeal by Sept. 30 — a date on the top of Paramount’s mind because it’s pledged to pay shareholders added “ticking fee” compensation amounting to about $7 million per day if the deal isn’t closed by then.
But the states called such a timeline unprecedented and unfair. They maintain that money Paramount might have to incur after Sept. 30 was the risk of a decision the company made on its own — and argued in a hearing held Friday that commencing a trial in April 2027 would allow enough time to for discovery and presenting proper evidence.
Including billion of dollars in debt, Paramount’s proposed purchase of Warner is currently valued at nearly $111 billion based on outstanding shares.
Paramount has repeatedly pointed to tech and streaming companies’ growing reach across the entertainment industry — and argues that merging with Warner would particularly help it compete with bigger rivals like Netflix. Last week, it said that the states’ challenge would help shield Netflix and others “who have harmed the market for theatrical exhibition” from meaningful competition.
The states’ case doesn’t focus on streaming. It alleges that a combined Paramount-Warner merger violates federal antitrust law because of what the companies’ combined reach would become across three markets: theatrical movie distribution, theater releases of bigger blockbusters, specifically, and the licensing of basic cable channels.
Per their complaint, a combined Paramount-Warner could control nearly a third of both the theatrical film distribution market as well as basic cable programming. The states argued this would create a “media behemoth” with enough power to both raise prices for consumers and threaten workers’ wages, while reducing production and quality of content.
In terms of movie theater releases, Paramount’s lawyers have also pointed to a growing list of blockbuster hits from studios that are not in Hollywood’s “big five,” such as A24 and Amazon’s MGM. The states maintain that the core legacy studios — which beyond Warner and Paramount, include Disney, Universal and Sony — still make up the vast majority of the market.
How a court will evaluate Paramounts’ arguments versus the states’ claims has yet to be seen. But the legal fight marks a major roadblock in Paramount’s quest to close its deal with Warner. And drawn-out proceedings could be costly and at the very least delay the transaction further.
Beyond California, states joining Monday’s lawsuit include Arizona, Colorado, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon and Washington.
Outside of the U.S., Paramount has touted greenlights from regulators in countries like China, Canada and Australia. But regulatory reviews are also ongoing in the European Union and the U.K., which has separately suggested it may intervene.
Meanwhile, the Writers Guild of America hasfiled its own lawsuitseeking to block the merger. And Paramount faces additional legal fights, including from a shareholder accusing CEO David Ellison and his father Larry Ellison (who isputting up tens of billions of dollarsto back the deal for his son’s company) of promising “illegal, private benefits” to Trump in order to get the merger through.
Paramount has repeatedly dismissed such accusations, and the U.S. Justice Department maintained its review was not political.
Still, politics have come into question throughout Paramount’s quest for Warner. Beyond Trump’s relationship with the Ellison family, critics of the deal also stress what new ownership could mean for Warner assets like CNN — a network that has long attracted ire from the Republican president — particularly in light ofeditorial turmoil at Paramount-owned CBSsince Skydance’s takeover.
The contrast between the 12 states’ complaint, brought forth by all Democratic attorneys general, and the federal government’s blessing only underlines all of that further.
“It’s a political merger. It’s the product of the president’s engagement in private business affairs,” New York Attorney General Letitia James said on a webinar about the states’ case last week. She maintained the states had been successful in challenging the Trump administration before and “we’re not finished yet.”
